subject: Re: improving file read/write
body:
Hmm, that's odd. the movies are in TIFF, about 607 MB per file, 11520x8184 
pixels. any ideas where I can look for sources of problem?
Reza

On Tuesday, April 9, 2019 at 1:03:25 PM UTC-4, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>
> Hi Reza,
>
> what file format are the movies in? I'm getting 7.3 s for reading a 40 
> frame K3 movie compressed as TIFF off a single SSD.
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
> On Tuesday, April 9, 2019 at 6:36:05 PM UTC+2, Reza B wrote:
>>
>> Hi Dimitry,
>>
>> I was trying Warp on our first batch of K3 data. There are 60 frames as 
>> opposed to 40-50 which I used to have and of course the area is abotu 50% 
>> larger. The processing speed has come down to ~25 hour and it's all in file 
>> read write, about 40s for read, 23 s for write, other steps take 2-3 
>> seconds. The data is on 4 striped SSDs with 64 GB RAM and a 6 core Intel 
>> i7-6850 3.6 GHz. I was wondering if there's anything that can be optimized 
>> further to improve overall read write speeds. Thanks.
>>
>> Best,
>> Reza
>>
>
